sections.title: Inspection Limitations
sections.content:
Building Inspection Limitations

This document explains the limitations that apply to the
Building Inspection and Inspection Report.

The inspection is a visual assessment only and is limited by
the accessibility, visibility and condition of the property at
the time of inspection.

1. Visual Inspection Only

The inspection is limited to visible and readily accessible
areas.

No destructive testing is undertaken.

The Inspector will not remove wall linings, floor coverings,
ceiling linings, insulation, furniture, stored goods,
landscaping or finishes to inspect concealed components.

Hidden defects may exist that cannot reasonably be identified
during a visual inspection.

 
  2. Readily Accessible Areas

The inspection is limited to areas that were safe and readily
accessible at the time of inspection.

Areas may be inaccessible due to:

Locked rooms

Stored goods

Furniture

Floor coverings

Wall linings

Ceiling linings

Insulation

Roof pitch

Limited roof access

Limited subfloor clearance

Safety risks

Building design

Items concealed behind finishes or construction materials
cannot be inspected and are therefore excluded from the scope
of the inspection.

3. Roof Spaces

Roof spaces are inspected only where safe access is available.

The Inspector will not enter roof spaces where access is
unsafe or where there is a risk of injury or property damage.

Examples include:

Unsafe framing

Excessive insulation

Restricted access openings

Electrical hazards

Excessive temperatures

Insufficient clearance

4. Subfloor Areas

Subfloor areas are inspected only where safe and practical.

Inspection may be restricted where there is:

Insufficient clearance

Standing water

Stored materials

Electrical hazards

Animal activity

Unsafe structural conditions

Where access restrictions exist, defects may remain concealed
and cannot be reported.

5. Weather Conditions

Weather conditions at the time of inspection may influence the
ability to identify defects.

Rain, recent rainfall, excessive moisture, wind or poor
lighting conditions may reduce the effectiveness of the
inspection.

Some defects may only become apparent under different weather
conditions or after prolonged occupation.

 
  6. Concealed Defects

The inspection cannot identify defects that are concealed by
building materials, finishes, fixtures, furniture, stored
items, vegetation or any other obstruction.

Examples include:

Hidden structural damage.

Defective framing concealed within walls.

Concealed moisture ingress.

Leaks within wall cavities.

Concealed plumbing defects.

Hidden electrical defects.

Insulation-covered defects.

Subsurface movement.

The absence of reported defects does not indicate that hidden
or concealed defects do not exist.

7. Occupied Properties

Where the property is occupied, inspection may be restricted
by furniture, stored belongings, floor coverings, personal
items and limited access provided by the occupant.

The Inspector is not required to move furniture, appliances,
stored goods or personal belongings.

Areas that cannot be inspected due to these restrictions are
excluded from the inspection.

8. Stored Goods and Obstructions

Inspection cannot be carried out where building components are
obstructed by stored goods, shelving, heavy furniture,
appliances, vegetation or other materials.

The Inspector accepts no responsibility for defects concealed
by such obstructions.

9. Services Excluded

Unless specifically agreed in writing, the following services
are excluded from the inspection:

Electrical installation testing.

Gas installation testing.

Plumbing system testing.

Hot water system performance.

Air conditioning systems.

Security systems.

Solar systems.

Communications systems.

Underground services.

Separate specialist inspections should be obtained where the
condition or performance of these services is important to the
Client.

 
  10. Environmental Conditions

The inspection does not include assessment of environmental
conditions or contaminants unless specifically agreed in
writing.

Examples include:

Asbestos-containing materials.

Lead-based paint.

Mould identification or laboratory testing.

Air quality.

Soil contamination.

Hazardous chemicals.

Radon gas.

Underground contamination.

11. Future Performance

The inspection represents the condition of the property only
at the date and time of inspection.

No guarantee or warranty is provided regarding the future
performance, deterioration or serviceability of any building
component.

Defects may develop after the inspection due to weather,
ground movement, age, normal wear and tear, maintenance,
building alterations or other causes beyond the Inspector's
control.

12. Further Investigation

Where visible evidence indicates possible structural issues,
water ingress, movement or other significant concerns, the
Inspector may recommend further investigation by an
appropriately qualified specialist.

Such recommendations should be completed before exchanging
contracts or proceeding with the purchase wherever practical.

Failure to obtain recommended specialist advice may expose the
Client to defects, repair costs or risks that were outside the
scope of this visual inspection.

13. Client Acknowledgement

By proceeding with the inspection, the Client acknowledges
that they have read and understood these Inspection
Limitations and accept that the inspection is restricted to a
visual assessment of the readily accessible areas of the
property.

The Client understands that concealed defects, inaccessible
areas and excluded items cannot be reported upon.

14. Applicable Standard

This Building Inspection has been undertaken generally in
accordance with Australian Standard AS 4349.1 – Inspection of
Buildings – Pre-Purchase Inspections – Residential Buildings,
except where otherwise agreed in writing.

End of Building Inspection Limitations

sections.title: Scope of Inspection
sections.content:
Scope of Building Inspection

This Building Inspection has been requested by the Client and
will be completed by SiteScop in accordance with Australian
Standard AS 4349.1 – Inspection of Buildings – Pre-Purchase
Inspections – Residential Buildings unless otherwise agreed
in writing.

1. Purpose of Inspection

The purpose of this inspection is to provide the Client with
an independent visual assessment of the condition of the
building at the date and time of inspection.

The inspection is intended to identify significant defects,
major defects, safety hazards and conditions requiring
further investigation.

 
      1. Purpose of Inspection

The purpose of this inspection is to provide the Client with
an independent visual assessment of the condition of the
building at the date and time of inspection.

The inspection is intended to identify significant defects,
major defects, safety hazards and conditions requiring
further investigation.

 
      2. Scope of Inspection

The inspection is a non-invasive visual assessment of the
readily accessible areas of the property.

The inspection does not include dismantling, removing,
lifting, cutting, excavating, testing, drilling or otherwise
damaging any part of the building.

Only areas that were safe and reasonably accessible at the
time of inspection are included.

Readily Accessible

A readily accessible area is an area that can be safely
inspected without the use of destructive methods,
specialist equipment or unreasonable risk.

3. Areas Included

Where safely accessible, the inspection may include the
following components:

Site and surrounding grounds

Foundations

Footings (visible only)

External walls

Internal walls

Floors

Ceilings

Roof exterior

Roof space

Subfloor

Windows

External doors

Internal doors

Garage

Carport

Balconies

Verandahs

Pergolas

Retaining walls (where applicable)

Paths and driveways

Drainage observations

Moisture observations

Visible structural elements

4. Areas Excluded

The inspection does not include any area that was:

Locked

Inaccessible

Unsafe

Obstructed

Concealed by furniture, storage or finishes

Covered by insulation

Below ground level

Inside wall cavities

Inside ceilings

Inside floor cavities not accessible

Services concealed within the building

Areas requiring destructive inspection

 
5. Defects Assessed During the Inspection

Major Defects

A major defect is a defect that requires significant repair,
replacement or further investigation. Major defects may affect
the structural integrity, safety, serviceability or durability
of the building.

Minor Defects

Minor defects generally include normal wear and tear,
maintenance items, cosmetic deterioration and minor damage
that does not significantly affect the performance of the
building.

Safety Hazards

The inspection includes visible safety hazards observed at the
time of inspection. Examples include loose balustrades,
trip hazards, damaged stairs, exposed electrical components,
unsafe handrails and other visible hazards.

Structural Observations

The inspection includes visual observations of structural
elements including movement, cracking, settlement,
deformation, deterioration and other visible signs that may
indicate structural concerns.

The inspection is not a structural engineering assessment.

Moisture Observations

Where moisture staining or evidence of water ingress is
observed, the inspector may use a non-invasive moisture meter
to assist with the assessment.

Moisture readings are indicative only and do not identify the
source or cause of moisture.

6. Inspection Method

The inspection is completed using a visual examination of
accessible building elements together with professional
judgement, experience and standard inspection tools.

Equipment may include:

High-powered inspection torch

Moisture meter

Thermal imaging camera (where applicable)

Digital camera

Ladder (where safe and practical)

Measuring devices

No destructive testing is undertaken unless separately agreed
in writing.

Important

The inspection represents the condition of the property only
at the time of inspection. Future deterioration, movement,
weather events or changes to the property are outside the
scope of this inspection.

 
     7. Items Specifically Excluded

Unless specifically agreed in writing, this inspection does
not include assessment of:

Hidden or concealed defects.

Footings below ground level.

Underground drainage systems.

Electrical installations.

Gas installations.

Plumbing systems.

Air conditioning systems.

Swimming pools and spas.

Solar systems.

Security systems.

Appliances and equipment.

Building compliance or certification.

Environmental hazards.

Asbestos identification.

Lead paint.

Mould testing.

Geotechnical conditions.

Boundary surveys.

Valuation of the property.

Important

The absence of reported defects does not guarantee that
defects do not exist. Concealed defects may be present that
cannot be detected during a visual inspection.

8. Recommendations

Where evidence of significant defects, deterioration,
structural movement or conditions requiring specialist
assessment is observed, the Inspector may recommend further
investigation by an appropriately qualified specialist before
the property is purchased or occupied.

Recommendations may include consultation with:

Structural Engineer

Licensed Electrician

Licensed Plumber

Roofing Contractor

Waterproofing Specialist

Drainage Specialist

Geotechnical Engineer

Other suitably qualified trades or consultants

9. Client Acknowledgement

By proceeding with this inspection, the Client acknowledges
that they have read and understood the Scope of Inspection and
accept that the inspection is limited to a visual assessment
of the readily accessible areas of the property at the time of
inspection.

The Client further acknowledges that no warranty or guarantee
is provided that every defect or condition will be identified.

10. Applicable Standard

This inspection has been prepared generally in accordance with
Australian Standard AS 4349.1 – Inspection of Buildings –
Pre-Purchase Inspections – Residential Buildings, except where
otherwise agreed in writing with the Client.

End of Scope of Building Inspection

sections.title: Terms and Conditions
sections.content:
Building Inspection Terms & Conditions

These Terms and Conditions form part of the Inspection Agreement
between SiteScop Pty Ltd ("the Inspector") and the
Client.

They govern the provision of the Building Inspection Service and
should be read together with the Scope of Inspection, Inspection
Limitations, Client Declaration and Privacy Policy.

1. Definitions

For the purposes of this Agreement:

Inspector means SiteScop Pty Ltd or its authorised representative carrying out the inspection.

Client means the person or entity requesting the inspection.

Property means the building or buildings nominated by the Client.

Inspection means the visual inspection carried out in accordance with the agreed scope.

Report means the written Building Inspection Report provided following the inspection.

2. Inspection Agreement

The Client engages SiteScop Pty Ltd to undertake a visual Building
Inspection of the nominated property.

The inspection will generally be completed in accordance with
Australian Standard AS 4349.1 – Inspection of Buildings –
Pre-Purchase Inspections – Residential Buildings unless otherwise
agreed in writing.

The inspection is a visual assessment only and does not constitute
a guarantee, certification or warranty regarding the condition of
the property.

3. Inspection Service

The Inspector will exercise reasonable care and professional skill
when conducting the inspection.

The inspection is limited to the readily accessible areas of the
property and to conditions visible at the time of the inspection.

No destructive testing or dismantling of building elements will be
performed unless separately agreed in writing.

 
  4. Fees and Payment

The Client agrees to pay the inspection fee advised by
SiteScop Pty Ltd before, on, or as otherwise agreed at the
time of booking.

Unless otherwise agreed in writing, the Inspection Report may
be withheld until payment has been received in full.

Additional fees may apply where:

The inspection scope is changed after booking.

Additional buildings or structures are requested.

Return visits are required due to restricted access.

Travel outside the agreed service area is required.

Specialist inspections are requested.

5. Access to the Property

The Client is responsible for ensuring that reasonable access
to the property is available at the agreed inspection time.

If access cannot be obtained due to locked areas, occupants,
animals, safety concerns or any other restriction, the
inspection may be delayed, limited or rescheduled.

Additional fees may apply where a further inspection is
required because access was unavailable.

6. Client Responsibilities

The Client acknowledges and agrees to:

Provide accurate booking information.

Nominate the correct property address.

Advise of any known hazards.

Ensure safe access to the property.

Read the Inspection Report in full.

Seek specialist advice where recommended.

Not rely upon verbal comments in place of the written report.

The written Inspection Report forms the complete professional
opinion of the Inspector. Verbal comments are general in
nature and must not be relied upon independently of the
written report.

7. Inspector Responsibilities

SiteScop Pty Ltd will perform the inspection using reasonable
care, professional skill and industry knowledge consistent
with the agreed scope of inspection.

The Inspector will report visible defects identified during
the inspection together with recommendations considered
appropriate at the time of inspection.

The Inspector is not responsible for identifying defects that
were concealed, inaccessible or outside the agreed scope of
inspection.

8. Variations to the Inspection

Any variation to the agreed inspection scope must be accepted
by both the Client and SiteScop Pty Ltd before additional work
is undertaken.

Variations may result in additional fees and revised reporting
timeframes.

 
  9. Limitation of Liability

The Inspection Report is prepared solely for the Client named
within the Inspection Agreement and only for the purpose for
which the inspection was commissioned.

The inspection is limited to a visual assessment of the
readily accessible areas of the property at the time of
inspection.

SiteScop Pty Ltd does not warrant that every defect,
deficiency or condition affecting the property will be
identified.

Hidden, latent or concealed defects may exist that are not
reasonably detectable during a visual inspection.

The absence of a reported defect does not guarantee that the
building is free from defects.

10. Third Party Reliance

The Inspection Report is prepared exclusively for the Client.

No other person or organisation may rely upon the Inspection
Report without the prior written consent of SiteScop Pty Ltd.

SiteScop Pty Ltd accepts no responsibility for any loss,
damage, cost or claim arising from reliance on the Inspection
Report by any third party.

11. Report Validity

The Inspection Report represents the visible condition of the
property only at the date and time of the inspection.

The condition of the property may change due to weather,
occupation, maintenance, deterioration, movement, repairs,
renovations or other events occurring after the inspection.

The Inspection Report should not be relied upon indefinitely
and should be considered current only as at the inspection
date.

12. Copyright and Intellectual Property

The Inspection Report, photographs, diagrams, thermal images,
notes and all associated documentation remain the intellectual
property of SiteScop Pty Ltd.

The Client receives a licence to use the Inspection Report for
its intended purpose only.

The Inspection Report must not be reproduced, altered,
published, distributed or used for commercial purposes without
the prior written consent of SiteScop Pty Ltd.

13. Recommendations

Where the Inspector recommends further investigation,
specialist assessment or additional inspections, the Client
acknowledges that such recommendations should be completed by
appropriately qualified persons before relying upon the
condition of the property.

Failure to obtain recommended specialist advice may expose the
Client to defects, repair costs or risks that fall outside the
scope of the visual inspection.

  
 14. Cancellation and Rescheduling

The Client may cancel or reschedule an inspection by providing
reasonable notice before the scheduled inspection time.

Where an Inspector has already attended the property or travel
has commenced, SiteScop Pty Ltd reserves the right to charge a
reasonable cancellation or attendance fee.

If the inspection cannot proceed due to circumstances beyond
the Inspector's control, including unsafe conditions, denied
access or severe weather, the inspection may be postponed or
rescheduled.

15. Complaints and Dispute Resolution

If the Client has any concern regarding the Inspection Report
or the inspection service, SiteScop Pty Ltd should be notified
in writing as soon as reasonably practicable after the concern
is identified.

Both parties agree to make reasonable efforts to resolve any
dispute through good faith discussions before commencing legal
proceedings.

Nothing in this Agreement limits any rights or remedies that
cannot lawfully be excluded under applicable Australian law.

16. Governing Law

This Inspection Agreement is governed by the laws of the State
or Territory in which the inspection is undertaken.

Any legal proceedings relating to this Agreement shall be
subject to the jurisdiction of the appropriate Australian
courts.

17. Acceptance of these Terms

By accepting the Client Inspection Agreement and proceeding
with the inspection, the Client confirms that they:

Have read these Terms and Conditions.

Understand the scope and limitations of the inspection.

Have had the opportunity to ask questions before accepting the agreement.

Agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions.

Acknowledge that the Inspection Report should be read together with all accompanying documents.

These Terms and Conditions form part of the complete SiteScop
Client Inspection Agreement and should be read together with
the Scope of Inspection, Inspection Limitations, Client
Declaration and Privacy Policy.

End of Building Inspection Terms & Conditions

sections.title: Privacy Policy
sections.content:
Privacy Policy

SiteScop Pty Ltd is committed to protecting the privacy of our
clients and handling personal information in accordance with
the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and the Australian Privacy
Principles (APPs).

This Privacy Policy explains how personal information is
collected, used, stored and disclosed when providing Building
Inspection services.

1. Information We Collect

Depending on the services requested, we may collect:

Client name.

Postal address.

Inspection property address.

Email address.

Telephone and mobile numbers.

Inspection booking information.

Inspection reports.

Photographs and thermal images.

Electronic signatures.

Communication records.

Payment information (where applicable).

2. How Information is Collected

Personal information may be collected through:

Inspection booking forms.

Our website.

Email correspondence.

Telephone conversations.

SMS communications.

Inspection Agreement forms.

Inspection reports.

Face-to-face meetings.

Only information reasonably necessary to provide our services
is collected.

  3. How We Use Your Information

SiteScop Pty Ltd collects and uses personal information only
for purposes reasonably necessary to provide our inspection
services.

Your information may be used to:

Arrange inspection appointments.

Conduct Building Inspections.

Prepare and deliver Inspection Reports.

Communicate regarding bookings and inspections.

Issue invoices and receive payments.

Respond to enquiries and customer support requests.

Meet legal and regulatory obligations.

Improve our services and internal systems.

4. Disclosure of Personal Information

SiteScop Pty Ltd does not sell or rent personal information to
third parties.

Information may be disclosed where reasonably necessary to:

Professional advisers.

Cloud storage providers.

Email and communication providers.

Payment service providers.

Government authorities where required by law.

Courts or regulatory bodies where legally required.

Personal information will only be disclosed where permitted or
required by Australian law.

5. Storage and Security

Reasonable administrative, technical and physical safeguards
are used to protect personal information against loss,
unauthorised access, misuse, disclosure or alteration.

Personal information may be stored electronically using secure
systems and cloud services.

Access is restricted to authorised personnel who require the
information to perform their duties.

6. Retention of Information

Inspection records, reports and related documentation may be
retained for as long as reasonably necessary to satisfy
business, insurance and legal obligations.

When information is no longer required, reasonable steps will
be taken to securely destroy or de-identify it.

7. Access and Correction

Clients may request access to personal information held by
SiteScop Pty Ltd and may request correction of inaccurate,
incomplete or outdated information, subject to applicable
Australian law.

8. Website and Cookies

Our website may use cookies and similar technologies to
improve functionality, analyse website usage and enhance the
user experience.

Users may disable cookies through their browser settings,
although some website features may not function correctly.

9. Changes to this Privacy Policy

SiteScop Pty Ltd may update this Privacy Policy from time to
time.

The latest version published forms the current Privacy Policy.

10. Contact Us

If you have questions regarding this Privacy Policy or how
your personal information is handled, please contact:

SiteScop Pty Ltd

Email: info@sitescop.com.au

Website: www.sitescop.com.au

End of Privacy Policy

sections.title: Client Declaration
sections.content:
Client Declaration

This declaration forms part of the SiteScop Client Inspection
Agreement and must be read together with the Scope of
Inspection, Inspection Limitations, Terms & Conditions and
Privacy Policy.

1. Client Acknowledgement

By accepting this Agreement and providing an electronic
signature, the Client acknowledges that they have read,
understood and accepted all documents forming part of the
Inspection Agreement.

I have read the Scope of Inspection.

I have read the Inspection Limitations.

I have read the Terms & Conditions.

I have read the Privacy Policy.

I understand the inspection is visual only.

I understand inaccessible areas cannot be inspected.

I understand concealed defects may exist.

I understand recommendations should be followed where applicable.

The Client confirms that sufficient opportunity has been
provided to review all Inspection Agreement documents before
accepting this Agreement.

2. Inspection Authority

The Client authorises SiteScop Pty Ltd to carry out the
requested Building Inspection at the nominated property in
accordance with the agreed scope of inspection.

 
  3. Electronic Signature

The Client acknowledges that an electronic signature,
including a signature created using a mouse, finger or stylus,
constitutes the Client's acceptance of this Inspection
Agreement.

The Client agrees that the electronic signature has the same
legal effect as a handwritten signature to the extent
permitted by applicable Australian law.

4. Accuracy of Information

The Client confirms that the information provided to SiteScop
Pty Ltd, including contact details and property details, is
true and correct to the best of their knowledge.

The Client agrees to promptly notify SiteScop Pty Ltd if any
material information changes before the inspection is carried
out.

5. Inspection Report

The Client understands that the Inspection Report is prepared
solely for the Client named within this Agreement and only for
the purpose for which the inspection was commissioned.

The Inspection Report must be read in its entirety together
with all photographs, recommendations and supporting
documentation.

The Client understands that recommendations for further
investigation should be completed by appropriately qualified
specialists before relying upon the condition of the property.

6. Privacy Consent

The Client consents to SiteScop Pty Ltd collecting, storing
and using personal information for the purposes of booking,
conducting and administering the inspection, preparing the
Inspection Report and communicating with the Client regarding
the inspection.

Personal information will be handled in accordance with the
SiteScop Privacy Policy.

7. Acceptance of Agreement

By signing this Agreement, the Client confirms that they have:

Read the complete Inspection Agreement.

Understood the Scope of Inspection.

Accepted the Inspection Limitations.

Accepted the Terms and Conditions.

Read the Privacy Policy.

Authorised SiteScop Pty Ltd to carry out the inspection.

Provided informed consent before the inspection commences.

The Client should not sign this Agreement unless they have had
sufficient opportunity to read and understand all Inspection
Agreement documents.

If the Client does not understand any part of this Agreement,
they should seek clarification before signing.

End of Client Declaration
